TextENGNew: When she opened her eyes, an unfamiliar ceiling filled her vision.
The faint smell of antiseptic. The cool stillness of an indoor space.
Sunlight streamed through the window, and she could hear sparrows chirping outside... From this, Tomoe gathered that it was early morning.
"Where... am I?"
When she shifted, she felt something cold against the back of her head. ...An ice pillow.
changeReason: Old says 'her back' but 後頭部 means 'back of the head' -- wrong body part.

TextENGNew: Growing slightly more alert, Tomoe sat up in the bed and looked around.
But... a dull pain lanced through her skull, and a groan escaped her lips.
The sound seemed to reach someone — a silhouette behind the white curtain rose from a chair with a creak.
A young man in a white coat peered through the gap in the curtains.
"...Hey, finally awake, are we?"
"Don't worry.@k@vS11/10/420900004. This is the village clinic. I'm Dr. Irie — I'm the physician here."
"N-nice to meet you..."
The young doctor who had introduced himself as Irie returned her greeting with a warm smile.
(...Now that I think about it, I think I saw him when we stopped by the clinic.)
Only then did she think to look down at what she was wearing.
At some point, her suit had been replaced with a hospital gown.
"...Ah, please don't worry about that.@k@vS11/10/420900006. Our nurse changed your clothes.@k@vS11/10/420900007. I wasn't in the room."
changeReason: Old includes treatment details ('disinfecting wounds and injection of nutrients'); new replaces final sentence with 'I wasn't in the room' -- different information about the doctor's presence during the examination.

TextENGNew: "Ah... thanks."
"Just wait here a moment.@k@vS11/10/420900009. I'll go call Ooishi-san for you."
changeReason: Japanese 呼んできます means 'I'll go get/fetch someone'; old 'give a call' implies phoning, while new 'go call' correctly implies physically going to fetch the person.

TextENGNew: Dr. Irie said this, then drew the curtain back and left the room.
Gently, she rolled up her left sleeve.
Just as Dr. Irie had said, there was a bandage on her elbow — probably from a scrape when she fell — and nearby, the telltale mark of a needle injection.
...Before long, hurried footsteps approached, and the door was thrown open with little ceremony. From behind the curtain, Ooishi appeared.
"...Tomoe-chan, are you all right?!"
"Ooishi-san...?"
"Aah~, what a relief.@k@vS11/11/420300284. You weren't waking up, so I was starting to worry."
His face crumpled with relief and joy as he dropped heavily into the folding chair beside her bed. ...His eyes were bloodshot, with faint dark circles beneath them.
"...I'm sorry to make you worry."
"Oh no, your safety is more important than anything else.@k@vS11/11/420300286. If anything happened to you, Madoka-chan in Kakiuchi would kill me~, nfu fu!"
"You're exaggerating..."
Tomoe laughed, but inwardly, Ooishi disagreed.
Madoka might put on a cynical front, teasing and needling her sister at every opportunity — but in truth, she worried about Tomoe more than anyone, and was deeply proud of her.
If anything happened to Tomoe on his watch, Madoka would never forgive him.
"How are you feeling?@k@vS11/11/420300288. Any pain anywhere?"
"Well... my head aches a little, but nothing serious."
"Sleep deprivation.@k@vS11/10/420900011. And it looks like you've been overworking yourself as well.@k@vS11/10/420900012. Your body simply couldn't take any more."
changeReason: Old ends with 'You haven't been getting much sleep recently, have you?'; new replaces this with 'Your body simply couldn't take any more' -- a different medical observation.

TextENGNew: Being told that stung. With everything she had to do, she had been spending her nights in the duty room for days on end.
...When had she last gone home? A week ago, to pick up a change of clothes? No, it might have been two weeks.
"That won't do, Tomoe-chan.@k@vS11/11/420300290. I know you're busy, but you need to take sleep seriously."
"I-I realize that..."
She began to think it over. Even though she intended to take some time off during the long holiday... why was she here?
Grumbling to herself, Tomoe heaved a dejected sigh.
"She's not in any danger, is she, Irie-sensei?"
"Yes, most likely.@k@vS11/10/420900014. You do have a bump on your head, but it seems you just hit it when you fell so you should be fine."
"...When I fell?"
"That's good to hear.@k@vS11/11/420300293. Irie-sensei... Do you mind if I talk to Tomoe-chan alone for a little while?"
"Sure, go right ahead.@k@vS11/10/420900016. I'll be in the exam room next door. Call me if you need anything."
Dr. Irie left the room after saying that.
...Ooishi locked the door, then returned to his chair at Tomoe's bedside and sat down again.
"...I'm sorry about yesterday."
Tomoe stared vacantly after Ooishi's sudden apology.

"Tomoe-chan, I rushed to my car and flew to the phone booth as soon as I received your message, and found you collapsed inside.@k@vS11/11/420300296. ...Honestly, I was a little drunk, but the sight completely sobered me up."
"Sorry about that.@k@vS11/28/420100797. ...No way, Ooishi-san. Were you driving inebriated?"
"Na ha ha, it's all right.@k@vS11/11/420300298. There was another young man at the wheel, and he didn't drink a single drop."
...So, Ooishi had made preparations to pick her up in the event of an emergency.
And those preparations indicated how wary he was of the danger in Hinamizawa.

"Well, if you're concerned about that, it seems like your head has fully cleared up.@k@vS11/11/420300300. ...In which case, allow me to ask a question.@k@vS11/11/420300301. Tomoe-chan... what happened last night?"
"Last night...?!"
...That moment.
The last memory before Tomoe lost consciousness came back to her, and she immediately placed her hand against her neck.
It stung, like the skin was burning up.
And when she tightened it, the feeling of suffocation she felt when it was being squeezed back then resurfaced, so she jumped and started to feel nauseous.
(That's right! Back then...!)
Reina Ryuugu ambushed her and strangled her as she attempted to exit the telephone booth...
Tomoe desperately resisted, but the tremendous strength was too much to overcome, so the vision in front of her eyes went black... and then she lost consciousness.
But... why?
If that was the case, why was she still alive?
"Ahahahahahahahaha.@k@vS11/02/420700039. ...Die, you idiot.@k@vS11/02/420700040. Ahahahahahahaha, ahahahahahahahahahahahah!!!"
Her entire body began to shake as a chill ran through her.
...Reina Ryuugu was definitely trying to kill Tomoe last night.
Her intense hatred and violent murderous intent were plain to see, even in the dark night.
Her fingernails bit into Tomoe's throat hard enough to snap bones, and she was completely merciless.
(Then... why did she stop before killing me?)
Did she notice Ooishi coming and run away?
...No, there's no way they would've overlooked the signs of an attacker.
Then did she let Tomoe go because she wasn't worth killing?
...That too seemed unlikely. Reina Ryuugu was relentlessly attacking at the time, like a wild animal.
Then... why on earth did she do it?
As much as Tomoe thought it over, she still couldn't find a reason why her life was spared.
"? ...What's wrong, Tomoe-chan?"
"Oh, it's nothing.@k@vS11/28/420100803. ...It happened so suddenly and it was dark, so I didn't get a chance to see the attacker's face."
"Hoh... It's hard to believe someone could've gotten the better of a level three judo master like yourself, Tomoe-chan."
"...Kendo."
As she made that offhanded comment... Tomoe suddenly realized something suspicious.
Based on their encounter at the garbage mountain, Reina Ryuugu didn't look especially strong for someone her age.
In fact, even though Reina's school records showed she was fairly capable, Tomoe was even stronger at her age.
But despite that, she wasn't able to fight back at all during the attack...
(Come to think of it, it was like that in that one case, too...)
The violent attack at the Ibaraki school.

When Reina Ryuugu was walking down the hallways swinging a metal bat and smashing windows, they said that it took multiple people including the PE teacher to stop her... Thinking about that, it was an outrageous story.
Could a group of strong adults really struggle to stop a young girl on a rampage...?
"So, what are you going to do now?@k@vS11/11/420300305. Irie-sensei says you should stay in the hospital for another day to recover your physical strength."
"No, I'll get up.@k@vS11/28/420100807. This is not the time to be sleeping around."
"Is that so?@k@vS11/11/420300307. Understood.@k@vS11/11/420300308. In which case, would you like to return to Okinomiya Station once more?"
"What's the matter?"
"...I can't change my clothes with you standing there."
"Aah, don't mind me.@k@vS11/11/420300311. Please, go right ahead."
"I do mind!!"
"Na ha ha!@k@vS11/11/420300313. Please forgive me."
With that, Ooishi stepped outside the curtain and turned his back.
She was ready to pummel Ooishi if he attempted to peep at her. ...Thinking that, Tomoe picked up her blouse and skirt and started to change her clothes on her side of the curtain.
"...Tomoe-chan."
"...I'll smack you if you peek."
"I just got back from the hospital Reina Ryuugu visits from time to time.@k@vS11/11/420300316. ...Just as you thought, it's apparently a remote university hospital in Gogura."
Tomoe slipped her arm through the last sleeve on her jacket, then turned toward Ooishi on the other side of the curtain.
According to Ooishi, Reina Ryuugu had been witnessed several times at this hospital.
Her visits were irregular, so it could've simply been due to seasonal illness, but Tomoe found herself thinking it over...

A housewife who knew the girl just happened to run into her at the hospital and tried calling out to her twice, but even though she clearly noticed the woman, she ignored her as if she didn't know her.
"I had a friendly meeting with that housewife last night, and she was able to tell me a bit more on the subject."
"According to her, everyone thinks Reina Ryuugu is a cheerful and friendly girl...@k@vS11/11/420300319. But at that time, she felt different. The woman was worried that she wasn't feeling well."
"However, the investigation was handled by prefectural police HQ, so it was outside of the Okinomiya police department's jurisdiction.@k@vS11/11/420300321. So our hands were tied."
"It's almost impossible to issue a warrant, so our activities are severely limited.@k@vS11/11/420300323. ...Are you still planning to go, despite that?"
"Of course."
She answered immediately. ...Tomoe's fast and courageous judgment was the number one reason Ooishi kept his eyes on her.
"So... this is it?"
After stepping in front of the hospital, Tomoe pointed her finger and rang that out.
This was probably one of the largest-scale general hospitals in the Chubu region.
It was furnished with the latest equipment and technology, and thanks to that it was full of talented young staff.
However, any patient seeking treatment is required to present a letter of recommendation, so anyone coming here without one is typically referred to a secondary office.

But more importantly, they were extremely reliable when it came to patient care and protection of personal information. Even though it was a workday, the waiting room was packed with people waiting for exams.
"Now that's a thriving business.@k@vS11/28/420100817. ...Although, I'm not sure that's a good thing."
It's not particularly desirable for police and hospitals to be extremely busy.
Just then,.
There was a pharmacy just beyond the hallway of the waiting room.
A nurse at the window was looking down at a girl bowing her head, handing over a prescription.
"...Umm, thank you very much."
"Alright, got it.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_Huke2002. Here is your number.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_Huke2003. We'll call you as soon we can, so please wait in the reception area for a little while."
"Oh, okay..."
The girl bowed once more after taking the tag with a reference number.
...Her face looked familiar.
And then, she stopped in her tracks as soon as she saw Tomoe's face.
Tomoe was momentarily at a loss when she saw that expression, but she began smiling in the friendliest way possible.
"Hello. We meet again."
"Oh... H-hello."
The girl timidly ducked her head while greeting Tomoe in a frail voice.
She had a very uncomfortable expression, like she was on alert knowing that the police were around.
"Are you feeling unwell?"
"N-no... not really..."
"...Oh, sorry.@k@vS11/28/420100822. It was rude of me to ask that.@k@vS11/28/420100823. Thanks to my job, I have a tendency to say anything that comes to mind."
Even after responding in that jovial tone, the girl carefully studied Tomoe's complexion, then immediately turned and looked down without saying anything.
This was not the place to hold somebody up. ...With that thought in mind, Tomoe shrugged her shoulders and said, "See you later," then began to turn around.
Then,.
"Th-thank you, for earlier.@k@vS11/36/420600041. Even though you helped me, I never properly thanked you..."
"Oh... sure. No big deal..."
Tomoe was feeling lost about how to respond to that sudden expression of gratitude.
...Besides, Tomoe did hear her say "Thanks" back then. Why on earth did she insist on going beyond that to say it again?
"Plus, I was feeling confused and just wanted to run away...@k@vS11/36/420600043. I'm sorry for being so rude."
"Being rude...? I never thought that at all.@k@vS11/28/420100827. In fact, your behavior was perfectly natural."
"...You're not angry?"
"What do I have to be angry about?@k@vS11/28/420100829. I'm really glad to see you again."
"Thank goodness..."
The girl finally flashed a smile while breathing a sigh of relief.
Even being the same gender, Tomoe found her innocent childlike smile endearing.
"Anyway, this hospital is really crowded.@k@vS11/28/420100831. Have you been waiting a while?"
"Ah, I got here early so it was pretty quick.@k@vS11/36/420600047. The pharmacy does look a little crowded, though."
Tomoe followed the girl's gaze and looked at the pharmacy a short distance away. Indeed, there were about five people lined up in front of the window, waiting for prescriptions and medical exams.
"Well, it must to be boring to keep waiting.@k@vS11/28/420100833. ...Oh, there's a vending machine nearby.@k@vS11/28/420100834. Would you like a juice?"
"Huh? Oh, I'm ok. That's all right..."
"It's fine, it's fine.@k@vS11/28/420100836. Which would you prefer, orange juice or soda?"
"Ah... O-orange juice."
As soon as Tomoe heard that, she put a coin in the nearby vending machine and pressed the orange juice button. Then she pressed it again and pulled out two bottles.
"Alright, how's this?"
"Ah, thank you.@k@vS11/36/420600051. ...Oh right, money."
"It's fine.@k@vS11/28/420100839. ...Or are you not going to drink the juice I bought?"
"Th-that's not it..."
"Ahahaha, I'm kidding.@k@vS11/28/420100841. It's cheaper than going to a coffee shop, so don't be shy."
"...Okay. Thank you."
Then, She pulled the tab and slammed down half her drink in one huge gulp.
The sweet smell and taste of citrus spread through her mouth and the cold sensation refreshingly ran down her throat. ...But simultaneously, her stomach started to say, "Give me more."
(Come to think of it, I came here straight from the clinic and didn't eat breakfast.)
In which case, maybe it would've been better to drink milk... Those thoughts nagged at Tomoe while she quickly gulped down the rest.
"Th-that was fast..."

"Hmm? Well, I've never lost to anyone on that front.@k@vS11/28/420100844. I'd spend lunchtime with the boys doing things like that, and my teachers would often scold me, telling me to "act more like a girl.""
"Ahaha...@k@vS11/36/420600056. Oh, thanks for the drink."
The girl relaxed her expression, then took a sip with both hands on the can.
Her mannerisms were like a cute little animal. Tomoe silently watched and smiled.
"...It's tasty."

"Yep, it's really good.@k@vS11/28/420100846. I was a little worried since I had never seen this manufacturer before, but this is a lucky break.@k@vS11/28/420100847. Maybe I should bring some back to Kakiuchi."
"Huh? You're from Kakiuchi?"
"Yep, that's right.@k@vS11/28/420100849. ...So, you too?"
"Oh, yeah.@k@vS11/36/420600060. I moved from Okinomiya about six months ago."
The girl went on to explain that she lived in a new residential area, not far from Tomoe's apartment.
"Wow, what a coincidence.@k@vS11/28/420100851. Actually, I only moved there from Ibaraki two months ago, so I guess that makes you my senpai."
"S-senpai? No way.@k@vS11/36/420600062. ...Oh, umm."
"...Oh right, my name is Minai.@k@vS11/28/420100853. Have you forgotten?"
"I-I'm sorry..."
The girl's shoulders slumped as she apologized for forgetting. ...She's a good kid.

"Ahahaha, don't worry about it.@k@vS11/28/420100855. There's no way you could remember someone's name after only hearing it once.@k@vS11/28/420100856. But I'd be thrilled if you called me Tomoe instead of referring to me by my last name."
"Tomoe...?"
"Yep, it's like the tomoenage throw in judo... even if that's a weird association.@k@vS11/28/420100858. I suppose that doesn't sound very sexy."
"Ah, ahahaha... ehe."
The girl bitterly smiled... then burst out as though she suddenly remembered something. Perhaps that "exaggerated demeanor" was something she came to associate with Tomoe.
"Ah... Okay, is it all right to call you Tomoe-san?"
"Sure. And may I ask your name?"
"Sure. My name is Natsumi.@k@vS11/36/420600068. Natsumi Kimiyoshi."
"Aah... then is it ok to call you Kimiyoshi-san?"
"Oh, please call me Natsumi."
"Sure, got it.@k@vS11/28/420100862. Natsumi-san, then?"
"Yeah."
After being referred to by her first name, the girl... Natsumi lost all the tension on her face and donned a gentle smile.
(Huh? Did she say Kimiyoshi...?)
That moment, Tomoe got stuck on that last name and started thinking it over. But Natsumi called her name, so she immediately went back to normal.
"What's wrong, Natsumi-san?"
"Umm... how is Kakiuchi?"
"How... is it?"
"You mentioned earlier,@k@vS11/36/420600073. ...you recently moved there, Tomoe-san, so I was wondering what kind of image you had of it?"

"Image, huh...?@k@vS11/28/420100867. The area near the station is lively, and it seems like there are a lot of restaurants.@k@vS11/28/420100868. But I'm living on my own, so I don't visit them very often."

"...@k@vS11/36/420600075.Coming from Okinomiya, it was really surprising for me.@k@vS11/36/420600076. I felt a little lost in the city when I first moved here... but I guess you could say it exceeded my expectations."
"...Exceeded your expectations?"
"On how different it is from this town..."
Natsumi's expression clouded over as she said that. ...Then, she played with the can in her hands while carefully choosing her words.
"Shopping in Kakiuchi is really convenient. It's lively and full of people.@k@vS11/36/420600079. ...But the speed of life here is so different, sometimes it makes my eyes spin."
"I'm not that good at following the latest and greatest in fashion..."
"Do you not enjoy your current lifestyle...?"

"No, that's not it.@k@vS11/36/420600082. There are so many places to hang out, and shopping is really fun.@k@vS11/36/420600083. ...But it's a completely different world from Okinomiya, so I was worried a country girl like me wouldn't be able to fit in..."
"...I see."
...The conversation started out with good momentum, but Natsumi slowly started confessing her troubles to Tomoe.
Even though this was their first real conversation. It was a strange feeling. ...But still, maybe they were on similar wavelengths.
And above all, they had a strange sense of kinship since they both recently moved to the same town...
It's natural to be confused by the differences when moving to a new environment. Even a slight change in lifestyle and dialect can add up to a major sense of incongruity for the person in question.
Of course, some people are able to accept those differences quickly. But for those who can't, that sense of incongruity accumulates and unwittingly causes them stress.
As a result, they close themselves off to stop those uncomfortable feelings, while continuing to conceal their anxiety...
(Is this girl more the latter case?)
And in most cases, it's difficult for those around them to notice that. ...Because for them, doing so is perfectly "natural."
As a result, the fatigue of repeated exertion builds up without anyone being aware of it, even the person themself, and it takes a toll on their mind and body.
Natsumi went on to explain she enrolled in a cram school right after moving to Kakiuchi.
Of course, the classwork and lifestyle were completely different from her earlier experience, so it took everything for her just to keep up with the daily course material.
During the flow of the conversation, Tomoe heard the name of Natsumi's high school. ...It was a famous school that even she had heard of.
"Mom recommended it.@k@vS11/36/420600087. She said going as hard as I can in high school will allow me to get into a good college."
"But even so..."
"...Indeed, that's like a person picking up running as a hobby, then going to a national contest without practicing.@k@vS11/28/420100874. Which would break first? Your legs or your heart?"
A change in living environment means a change in study environment. ...There's nothing unusual about that causing stress.
Plus, as someone who saw herself as a "country girl," she couldn't pass that responsibility off on others so she had to inflict it on herself.
She didn't have similar hobbies or things to talk about with her neighbors. She couldn't keep up with the classwork. ...And she felt it was her fault that she was different from everyone else.
A child like that doesn't even realize that they're accumulating stress, let alone understand how to vent that stress.
In which case, it really should be the responsibility of the surrounding adults to look after her...
"...Sorry, Tomoe-san.@k@vS11/36/420600090. I've been talking about weird things..."
"No, don't worry about it..@k@vS11/28/420100876. I completely understand how you feel."
"...Actually, I'm just like you.@k@vS11/28/420100878. I moved when I went to college, and I was feeling the same way you are right now."
"...Oh, really?"
Natsumi quickly began to look surprised as she heard that. Tomoe attempted to make her feel at ease by smiling and telling her an old story of her own.

"...I lived in Tokyo back in high school.@k@vS11/28/420100880. But I moved to Ibaraki due to family circumstances... and the rhythm of daily life completely changed on me. I was really confused.@k@vS11/28/420100881. It's the complete reverse of your situation, Natsumi-san."

"Looking back on it now, the difference wasn't actually that significant.@k@vS11/28/420100883. ...But the accent and manner of speaking were a little different, and those little things added up to heavy discomfort."
"Plus, you take for granted how easy it is to get things in Tokyo, but out there it's hard just to buy things.@k@vS11/28/420100885. ...I felt like I got left behind."
"You got left behind..."

"Plus, thanks to my grandmother's hip problems, I had to help with farming in the field.@k@vS11/28/420100887. I failed in all sorts of ways when I first started, and I was amazed by the other people doing the same work... Honestly, I hated it so much back when I wasn't used to it."
"Especially since I was rather conservative and struggled to adapt to new things.@k@vS11/28/420100889. ...I thought about going back to my old town, every single day."
"Tomoe-san..."
Not only that, but she lost her parents and her sister went missing...
Although she couldn't tell Natsumi that, but the sense of loss and stress at that time was far beyond student life.
How many times did she beg for those peaceful days to return...?
"Even so...?"

"When I ate vegetables that I grew myself for the first time, they were absolutely delicious.@k@vS11/28/420100893. Even though they were the same vegetables I bought in Tokyo, the taste was different.@k@vS11/28/420100894. ...It was strange."
It may have simply been that they were fresh.
Or they may have felt different because she made them with her own hands.
Despite such a minor difference... Tomoe was surprised by the result.
"...At that time, I felt it.@k@vS11/28/420100896. Not all the differences were bad.@k@vS11/28/420100897. Nevertheless, I was too focused on trying to fit in, too overly concerned, too egotistical."
"And I hated being different, instead of trying to enjoy the differences..."
"Enjoy... the differences?"

"That's right. Natsumi-san. Have you ever traveled?@k@vS11/28/420100900. It's just like that.@k@vS11/28/420100901. Seeing and enjoying an environment that's different from where you live.@k@vS11/28/420100902. ...Isn't that the best part of traveling?"
"But it's strange that we don't think about it that way when we move somewhere new.@k@vS11/28/420100904. ...Even though they're essentially the same thing."
"...That's because travel is only for a short time.@k@vS11/36/420600099. But when you move, you'll remain in that place forever."
After saying that in protest... Natsumi gasped. She seemed surprised at herself for having talked back.
"Oh... I-I'm sorry.@k@vS11/36/420600101. I didn't mean that."
"No, you're right about that.@k@vS11/28/420100906. Plus in your case, you have to consider school.@k@vS11/28/420100907. In that sense, it's not the same as traveling."
"However," Tomoe continued.
"When it comes to "desire," a single mistake can turn it into "obligation.""
"For example, at first you might think, "I want to do it," but before long it can drive you into, "I have to do it.""

"...I used to do high jump in track and field, but back then two different ways of thinking, "I want to break records," and, "I have to break records," had a completely different level of pressure to them."
"Of course, I can't say which of those is more correct, but being driven by obligation is certainly more stressful and more painful..."
Natsumi suddenly caught her breath, and her eyes opened wide.
Tomoe may have simply uttered a rule of thumb she learned through experience.
But for Natsumi... it perfectly encapsulated her personal suffering.
"Wanting to keep up" vs. "needing to keep up" in class.
"Wanting to get used to" city life vs. "needing to get used to" city life.
It could be nothing more than wordplay.
But even so... indeed, it reached directly into Natsumi's heart.
Before long, she started to feel the "obligation" to fit in to the city...
"...Oh, umm."
"Hmm?"
"So, in times like those... what are you supposed to do?"
Before she even realized it herself, she stepped forward and asked that.

Tomoe was puzzled for a moment by the intensity of her gaze... but somehow, she started to feel a sense of kinship to the girl, so she stopped for a moment to think earnestly, carefully choosing her words.
"I don't know if my personal experience will be any help, but... here's one way of thinking about it."

"Before you can say, "I have to get used to this," and, "I have to fit in," you need to think, "I want to get used to this," and, "I want to fit in."@k@vS11/28/420100915. For example, rather than saying, "I have to get along with my friends," it should be, "I want to get along with my friends.""
"Not that you have to... but you want to."
"Right.@k@vS11/28/420100917. ...By the way, Natsumi-san, have you made any friends in this new town yet?"
"Oh, yes.@k@vS11/36/420600107. But because the tempo is so different, it always seems like I'm barely keeping up..."
"But... how do you feel about it? Are you not having fun?"
"N-no, I wouldn't say that..."
Natsumi violently shook her head in response to that question.
...There was no reason to say she wasn't having fun.
Walking around town together while shopping and chatting while eating fast food were always new and interesting experiences.
There was a girl with braids who always raised the mood with her over-the-top antics.
When Natsumi was sitting alone in class and feeling left out, she called out to her from the other end of the room to look after her.
And standing next to her was the straight-man in their comedy duo, a kind-hearted girl like a caring older sister.
She often lent Natsumi her notes when she couldn't keep up in her studies.
And then, a quiet but gentle boy who drew lovely pictures...
Thinking about it, she was thankful to have such a happy environment.
However, that "gratitude" turned "apologetic"...
Every time she thought about it, she felt pathetic about how powerless she was.
Compared to those kind-hearted friends, she felt dazed, jealous... sometimes even envious.
It was getting harder and harder to tell them the truth...
"They're... fun.@k@vS11/36/420600110. They started talking to me on the first day I moved here, and they've been looking out for me in all sorts of ways."
"But, I wonder if that might actually be worse for everyone.@k@vS11/36/420600112. Because everyone does all kinds of things for me, but I can't do anything for them..."
"...Natsumi-san."
Tomoe could feel she really was a kindhearted girl.
She had been listening to Natsumi's story this whole time, but Natsumi never said anything bad about her friends or her parents.
Surely there was someone she could blame for her guilty feelings.
(But still...)
With things as they were, this girl was continuing to build up stress.
And pain and suffering... that could very well explode one day if she reaches her limit.
Tomoe had seen many cases of an earnest child falling into delinquency out of desperation, and she was worried about that.
So...
"Hey, Natsumi-san.@k@vS11/28/420100922. Do you think your friends do these things for you out of self-interest?"

"You hang out and have fun conversations with your friends.@k@vS11/28/420100924. If anything was upsetting you, they'd extend a helping hand.@k@vS11/28/420100925. And when that happens, do you think they're expecting anything in return?"
"...@k@vS11/36/420600115.No, nothing like that."
"Exactly.@k@vS11/28/420100927. They don't think that way at all, do they?@k@vS11/28/420100928. They're simply doing it because they want to."
"...Right."
"In which case, wouldn't it be best to accept their favors graciously?"
"I'd be happy to hear my friend say "thank you" instead of feeling apologetic.@k@vS11/28/420100931. That's the privilege of friendship. You share your troubles with one another."
"Share your troubles with one another?"
Natsumi's eyes went wide when she heard something she had never considered before.
They were her friends, so naturally she wanted to do something good for them... but the opposite of that was "privilege"...?
"Exactly. Because you definitely can't bother a stranger with problems like this, right?"

"Playfully beating someone, or saying awful things...@k@vS11/28/420100934. normally those would make someone angry, but that's something friends can do.@k@vS11/28/420100935. And if it's someone you really love, they'll turn it around on you."
"...Somehow... I see.@k@vS11/36/420600119. But... do my friends feel that way, too?"

"Of course.@k@vS11/28/420100937. If they didn't, they wouldn't be your friends.@k@vS11/28/420100938. In fact, if you're being especially apologetic or struggling to respond to them, it won't make them feel any better."
"Because otherwise, all the time you spend thinking you "have to" do things for your friends will only make it harder for you to stay together."
When Tomoe said that... Natsumi suddenly gasped.
Is that it...?
The source of her pain was that she was in a panic, feeling she "had to" do something for her kind friends.
So, if she didn't panic... surely it wouldn't be painful.
If she could just get back to her usual pace and interact with them like normal...
"Hmm?"

"Is that really all right?@k@vS11/36/420600124. Acting like usual around Chisato-chan and Tamako-chan...?@k@vS11/36/420600125. Oh, those are my friends' names. But if I do that, will we be able to get along well?"
"Good question.@k@vS11/28/420100942. I don't know these girls, so I can't make any promises..."

"However... even if they don't know you deep down, these kids are coming to you and making an effort to get along with you, right?@k@vS11/28/420100944. In which case, it's probably not as one sided as you telling them, "Come here.""
"I'm sure they noticed that you're carrying heavy baggage.@k@vS11/28/420100946. So, they called out to you saying, "Can I help?"@k@vS11/28/420100947. ...Don't you think so?"
Natsumi recalled when she first met the two of them.
Chisato said, "We're friends, so don't hold back."
Tamako said, "Feel free to speak up if anything is bothering you."
...But she was the one holding back.
She was the one who couldn't speak up.
The two of them always left the door open for her...

"If these kids are as kind as you say, it'll be fine.@k@vS11/28/420100949. If you really feel you want to get along with them, they'll understand when you talk to them.@k@vS11/28/420100950. So, why not try speaking with them?"
"Tell them, "From now on, I'll do my best to help out."@k@vS11/28/420100952. If you still feel the need to say "I'm sorry," maybe you should add this."
""I'll do everything I can for everyone else, too.""
"Tomoe-san..."
...It was strange.
Even though she had only just met Tomoe... her words penetrated deep into her heart.
Her parents and teachers had probably said similar things in the past... but as far as Natsumi was concerned, they spoke coldly... like they were discussing someone else's business.
But... strangely, she felt... that this woman was different...
There was no way to be certain, but for some reason she believed in her...
I'll give it a try.
Natsumi swore that in her heart... as she lifted her head.
Even she thought it strange how the weight on her shoulders felt lighter and her heart felt at ease.
"...Thank you, Tomoe-san."

"...I'll give it a try.@k@vS11/36/420600132. I don't know how well I can do it, but... I'll do my best to talk to my friends.@k@vS11/36/420600133. I'm a little nervous... but if I don't say anything, it'll never change."
"...These aren't just empty words, Natsumi-san.@k@vS11/28/420100956. If I was your friend, this is what I'd say.@k@vS11/28/420100957. "Don't worry about that. Let's do our best together.""
"...Ehehe."
With that, something seemed to get through to Natsumi, and she responded with a radiant smile.
The gloomy atmosphere up until this point had completely vanished.
Just then, a voice from behind the pharmacy counter called out, "Number 33, Natsumi Kimiyoshi-saaan."
"Ah, I need to go get that."
"Well, I should get going soon.@k@vS11/28/420100959. Sorry for taking up your time chatting about whatever I felt like."
"No, that's no problem.@k@vS11/36/420600137. ...Umm, would it be all right if we talked again some time?"
"Yes, of course.@k@vS11/28/420100961. Because... we're friends, right?"
"Y-yeah...!"
Natsumi nodded with a pleasant smile on her face.
"Oh, then..."
"What's it?"
"Sorry... but do you mind sharing your contact information?"
"Of course.@k@vS11/28/420100964. Hold on a second."
She pulled out a business card and wrote her home phone number on it. Then, Natsumi wrote her phone number down and the two of them exchanged papers.
"Oh, but if you tell anyone, "She's a police officer," it'll scare people.@k@vS11/28/420100966. So, what would be a better thing to say...?"
"Then say you're a library clerk.@k@vS11/36/420600143. I like books and read them all the time.@k@vS11/36/420600144. ...That seems like a good enough lie, don't you think?"
Tomoe responded, "Sounds good," to Natsumi's suggestion.
Then, Natsumi said, "Thank you," once again and bowed before running off to the pharmacy.
After seeing her off with a smile, She turned and headed toward the main office.
